templates.conflict1Text: I want to be sure before I let myself off the hook. Could you bring me a {quest_item}, the kind {settlement_npc} has been making lately? If I see it for myself, I'll know whether they're using my methods or something they figured out on their own. Either way I want to stop guessing.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: This isn't my work at all. They've moved past what I taught them and built something new on top of it, and here I was being jealous of someone who was honoring me by doing better than me. I don't know whether to laugh or to hide.
templates.resolutionText: I went and told {settlement_npc} I was proud of them, which is the conversation I should have had three weeks ago instead of stewing. They cried, which means I had hurt them more than I knew, and I hate that. Take {quest_reward}: you saved a friendship I was too small to save myself.
templates.rewardText: the best piece of my own work, since I'd like someone to remember how it looks

templates.expositionText: A friend of mine has been accused of stealing from a neighbor, and I know in my bones it wasn't them. They don't have it in them, and the timing doesn't add up no matter how I look at it. Would you go to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}? They were trading in {settlement_name} the day this all supposedly happened, and they'd know who was where.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. People around here have already made up their minds, and I was starting to feel like the only one willing to say wait a moment.
templates.declineText: So my friend gets to wear this for the rest of their life, then. I had hoped you of all people would understand why that matters, but I suppose I'll find someone else.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} confirmed it. My friend wasn't anywhere near it when it happened, and they've got the trading partner to vouch for it. That's a start, but a witness from another settlement isn't going to be enough on its own.
templates.conflict1Text: I need physical proof. There should be a {quest_item} that ties the actual thief to what they took: bring me one, and I'll have something concrete to show the people who've been pointing fingers. Right now it's word against word, and word is losing.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: This is exactly what I needed. Holding this, I can see how it all fits together, and now I know who actually did it. I almost wish I didn't, because the truth is going to upset some people who don't want to be upset.
templates.conflict2Text: I need {target_npc} to come back here and say what they told you, in front of the people who matter. Could you go ask them to make the trip? They won't want to: they'd rather stay out of our business. But they'll come if you tell them I'm asking and that I have proof now.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} agreed to come. That's everything. With their testimony and the evidence you found, there's no version of this where my friend stays accused. The truth is finally going to get its turn to speak.
templates.resolutionText: It's done. My friend's name is clear, and the people who were so quick to accuse them have been just as quick to look at their own boots. That's how it should be. Take {quest_reward}: you stood up for someone you'd never even met, and that's the kind of thing I want to remember about people.

skillCheck.passText: There's a piece I haven't told anyone yet. The person who first pointed at my friend had reasons of their own to want them gone, and I think the accusation was the lever, not the cause. I haven't said it out loud because I don't have proof, but I will by the time this is over.
skillCheck.failText: It's a complicated story and I don't have it all straight in my head yet.

templates.rewardText: what coin I have set aside and a friendship that owes you one too

templates.expositionText: People in {settlement_name} have decided I'm the one who broke a promise I never made, and I'm running out of ways to defend myself. There's a {quest_item} I gave away weeks ago, the same kind I'm now accused of hoarding: bring me one as proof that I never had what they say I had. I need something solid in my hand before anyone will listen to me.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've been talking myself hoarse and getting nowhere, and a stranger's word might land where mine can't.
templates.declineText: Of course. Why would anyone help me when the whole settlement has already decided who I am. Forget I asked. I'll keep shouting at walls until my voice gives out.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Holding this is the first piece of evidence anyone has given me a chance to show. I feel like I can breathe a little. But evidence alone won't fix what people have been telling each other about me.
templates.conflict1Text: I need {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} to vouch for me. They were the one I gave the original to, weeks ago, and they can tell anyone who'll listen that the story being passed around is wrong. Could you ask them to speak up for me? They don't owe me anything, but they're a fair person, and that's what I'm counting on.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} is willing to come and say it plainly. I almost wept when you told me. I haven't had someone in my corner in weeks, and now I've got two of you, and that's more than I had any right to expect.
templates.resolutionText: {target_npc} came and said their piece, and the people who'd been so sure of themselves had nothing to say back. My name is mine again. Take {quest_reward}: you and {target_npc} gave me something I didn't think I'd get back, and I'm going to remember who showed up when nobody else would.

skillCheck.passText: I'll tell you what I haven't told anyone. I almost left {settlement_name} last week. I had my pack at the door and I was going to walk out and let them think whatever they wanted. The only reason I'm still here is that I decided I'd rather be cleared than be free.
skillCheck.failText: It's been a hard few weeks. I'd rather not get into it.

templates.rewardText: a share of my own stores and the kind of thanks I don't usually give freely

templates.expositionText: Someone I care about has been blamed for damage they couldn't possibly have done, and the case against them keeps getting louder while no one stops to check the facts. Would you start by talking to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}? They saw the damage before anyone else did, and they noticed something the rest of us missed.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've been carrying this alone for days and the weight of it was about to crush me. Knowing someone else thinks it's worth looking into is more than I can say.
templates.declineText: So we let it stand. We let everyone go on believing the easy story because the true one is too much work. I expected better, frankly, and I'm not going to pretend I didn't.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} told you about the marks on the wood? I knew there was something off about how the damage looked, and now I have a name for what made it. This was never something a person could have done.
templates.conflict1Text: I need to show people, not just tell them. Bring me a {quest_item}: it's the kind of thing that would have been left behind if my version of events is right. If we can put it in front of the accusers, the story falls apart on its own.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: This is exactly what I was hoping for. Anyone who looks at this and still wants to blame my friend is choosing to be wrong, and I can work with that.
templates.conflict2Text: I need one more piece. There should also be a {quest_item} that points to how the damage actually happened: that'll prove the cause, not just that my friend wasn't there. I want this airtight before I bring it to the people who matter.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Now I have the whole picture. The damage, the cause, the timeline: it all fits, and none of it points at the person they've been blaming. I'm almost looking forward to the conversation I get to have next.
templates.conflict3Text: Last thing. I need {target_npc} to come back to {settlement_name} with me and say what they saw, in person, in front of everyone who's been spreading the wrong story. Could you ask them once more? With everything we've gathered, they'll know it's worth the trip.
templates.conflict3TurnInText: {target_npc} is on their way. Between their testimony and what we've gathered, this is going to be the cleanest exoneration anyone in {settlement_name} has ever seen. My friend deserves nothing less.
templates.resolutionText: It's over. My friend has their good name back, and the people who were so eager to take it have learned to be slower with their accusations next time. That's the part I'm proudest of. Take {quest_reward}: you didn't just help me, you helped this place be a little fairer than it was yesterday.
templates.rewardText: what I can give from my own stores and the lasting thanks of the person you cleared

templates.expositionText: I said something to my sister that I shouldn't have, and I said it where everyone could hear. She hasn't spoken to me since, and she has every right. I want to make it right, but I can't just walk up to her empty-handed after what I did. If you can find me a {quest_item}, something with a little weight to it, I'll know how to use it.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I don't deserve the help, but I'll take it and try to be worth it.
templates.declineText: No, that's fair. I made this mess. I shouldn't expect anyone else to wade into it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You actually found one. That's a {quest_item} I can put in someone's hands and mean it. Thank you: now I have to do the harder part.
templates.conflict1Text: I can't bring this to her myself. Not yet. I need you to find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}: they used to know us both, before all this. If anyone can tell her I'm trying, it's them.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: You spoke to {target_npc}? And they didn't laugh you out of the room? That's more than I had any right to hope for.
templates.resolutionText: She came by this morning. Didn't say much. Just sat with me for a while, and that was more than I'd had in weeks. Take {quest_reward}: I owe you something I can't really name.

skillCheck.passText: The truth is, I knew exactly what I was doing when I said it. I wanted to hurt her in that moment, and I succeeded. That's the part I can't sleep on.
skillCheck.failText: It's complicated. Family things usually are.

templates.rewardText: what coin I've been setting aside, and a quiet thank-you I won't forget

templates.expositionText: There was a man who passed through last winter, and I turned him away when he asked for shelter. I had room. I just didn't want the trouble. I heard later he'd been a friend of someone I cared about, and I've carried it ever since. Please find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}: I think they knew him, and I need to know if he came through alright.
templates.acceptText: Thank you for not asking me to justify it. I couldn't if I tried.
templates.declineText: I understand. It's a strange thing to ask of a stranger. I'll keep carrying it, same as before.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} told you he made it through? I wasn't sure I wanted to know either way: knowing makes it real, but not knowing was eating me. Thank you for bringing me the truth.
templates.conflict1Text: Now I want to do something for him, even if he never hears about it. There's a {quest_item} I'd like to send back through {target_npc_settlement}: something practical, something that says I should have done better. Can you find one for me?
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's exactly the kind of {quest_item} I had in mind. He'll know it cost something to send. That matters.
templates.conflict2Text: Last thing: would you take it back to {target_npc} for me? I want them to give it to him, in their own words. I'm not ready to stand in front of him, but I can stand behind a gesture.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: You did it. You actually did it. I think I can breathe a little easier tonight.
templates.resolutionText: {target_npc} sent word back. He didn't say much, but he kept the gift, and that's all I asked for. Take {quest_reward}: you carried something for me that I couldn't carry alone.
templates.rewardText: the best of what I have left from that hard winter, and my honest thanks

templates.expositionText: I broke something that wasn't mine to break. It belonged to my father, and I let my temper get the better of me, and now there's a hole in my home where it used to sit. He's gone, so I can't apologize to him. The least I can do is replace it: bring me a {quest_item} and I'll start there.
templates.acceptText: You're being kinder than I've earned. I'll try to make this matter.
templates.declineText: I don't blame you. It's my mess. I'll get to it eventually, or I won't.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a fine {quest_item}. He would have approved of the craftsmanship, at least. Holding it makes the empty spot on the shelf feel like something I'm filling, not just hiding.
templates.conflict1Text: There's one more thing I need. He always kept a {quest_item} alongside it: said the two belonged together. If you can find one of those too, then the set will be whole again, the way he had it.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Both pieces, side by side. That's how he kept them. That's how I'll keep them. Thank you for not asking me why this mattered so much.
templates.resolutionText: I put them up tonight, in the same spot. It's not the same: they're not his. But the hole is gone, and that's something. Take {quest_reward}: it's the kind of thing he would have given you, if he were the one standing here.

skillCheck.passText: You're looking at the marks on my hand, aren't you? Yes, that was the same night. I haven't forgiven myself for that part either, but at least it heals.
skillCheck.failText: It's an old story. Not worth dragging through the mud again.

templates.rewardText: a small purse and the quiet thanks of a man trying to be better

templates.expositionText: My oldest friend won't see me, and I don't know why. I thought I was helping when I spoke to her family on her behalf, but something I said made everything worse. I've been turning it over in my head for days and I can't find the moment it broke. Please, talk to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}: she trusts them, and they might tell you what I did.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. Maybe a fresh pair of eyes will see what I keep missing.
templates.declineText: Of course. It's a tangle, and you have your own life. I'll just keep going in circles, I suppose.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} actually told you? Oh: oh, that's what she heard. I never meant it that way, not even close. But I see now how it sounded, and I can't unsay it.
templates.conflict1Text: Now that I know, I want to make it right. There's a {quest_item} she always loved, the kind we used to find together when we were small. If you can bring me one, I'll take it to her myself, and I'll have something to say with my hands when the words won't be enough.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's perfect. She'll know what it means the moment she sees it. Thank you for understanding why this had to be the right one.
templates.resolutionText: She let me sit with her this morning. We didn't talk about the thing I said, not yet, but she took the gift and she didn't put it down. That's a beginning. Take {quest_reward}: you gave me back something I was sure I'd lost.

skillCheck.passText: The honest part is, I was trying to protect her from her family, because I'd seen them be cruel to her before. I wasn't being a busybody. I was being scared for her, and I overstepped because of it.
skillCheck.failText: I'd rather not pick at it any more than I already have. It hurts enough.

templates.rewardText: a gift I'd been saving for a better day, and my honest gratitude

templates.expositionText: I gave my brother some advice last month that I really thought would help him, and instead it cost him work and pride and probably his patience with me. I still don't understand all of it. What I do know is that I owe him something more than another conversation about it. Find me a {quest_item}: something useful, something he'll actually want.
templates.acceptText: I appreciate it. I keep wanting to fix this with words, but words are what got me here.
templates.declineText: Yeah. I figured I'd have to muddle through this on my own. That's probably how it should be anyway.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a fine {quest_item}, and exactly the kind of thing he'd never buy for himself. He's stubborn that way. Holding it actually makes me feel like I might be able to face him.
templates.conflict1Text: But I can't just hand it over without help. I need you to find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} first: they were there when I gave him the advice, and they know what really happened. If they can tell me what I missed, I'll know what to say when I see him.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: So that's what he heard me saying. I thought I was offering options, and he heard me telling him he wasn't capable. {target_npc} did me a kindness telling you that. Now I know the apology I owe.
templates.resolutionText: I went to him last night. Brought the gift, said the right thing for once, and he laughed: a tired laugh, but a real one. We're not all the way back, but we're talking. Take {quest_reward}: this would have stayed broken without you.

skillCheck.passText: I'll tell you what I haven't told anyone: I'm afraid he'll never trust my word again. He used to come to me first when something was hard, and he hasn't since. That's the part that's breaking me.
skillCheck.failText: It's between brothers. I shouldn't have laid it all out like this.

templates.rewardText: a portion of what I've put aside this season, and a brother's thanks

templates.expositionText: My daughter has been quiet around me for two weeks now and I don't know which thing I did wrong. I've replayed every conversation and there are three or four moments where I might have hurt her without seeing it. I can't ask her: she'll just say it's nothing. Please, find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}, the friend she writes to: she'll have told them the truth.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I'd rather know than keep guessing wrong.
templates.declineText: I understand. It's a parent's mess, and parents are supposed to figure these things out themselves. I'll keep trying.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} told you it was the comment about her work? I thought I was encouraging her. She heard it as me saying she wasn't enough. Of course that's what she heard: I should have known.
templates.conflict1Text: I want to show her I see her differently than she thinks I do. There's a {quest_item} she's been wanting for a long time, and I kept telling her it wasn't practical. That was wrong of me. Bring me one, and I'll give it to her without a single condition attached.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Look at this. She'll know what it cost me to choose this for her, and she'll know it's not about being practical. That's the whole point. Thank you.
templates.conflict2Text: One more favor. Would you take word back to {target_npc} that I'm grateful, and that I'm trying? They've been a better friend to my daughter than I knew, and they should hear it from someone besides me.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} took the message well? Good. I want her friend to know I'm not the kind of mother who'd resent her for being honest with you.
templates.resolutionText: She opened the gift in front of me. She didn't say much, but she sat down next to me, and she stayed. That's the most I've had from her in weeks. Take {quest_reward}: you walked through something with me that I couldn't have walked alone.
templates.rewardText: a parcel of good food from my pantry and a mother's quiet thanks

templates.expositionText: There's someone I think about more than I should admit, and I have never been able to put it into words around them. Every time I try, my tongue ties itself in knots. I think if I had something to give them, the right thing, I could let the gift do the talking. Please, find me a {quest_item}: nothing flashy, just something that means something.
templates.acceptText: Oh, that's a relief. And also kind of terrifying, now that I've actually said it out loud.
templates.declineText: No, I get it. It's a silly thing for a grown person to be tangled up over. I'll keep tying my own knots, then.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Look at it. That's exactly the kind of {quest_item} I was picturing without knowing how to picture it. Now I just have to not drop it on the way there.
templates.conflict1Text: One more thing, and this one I really can't do alone. They moved to {target_npc_settlement} last spring, and I haven't worked up the nerve to visit. Could you go to {target_npc} for me and just: tell them I've been thinking about them? Pass the gift along if it feels right.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} smiled? You're sure? You're not just being kind to me? That's: that's more than I let myself hope for, walking out the door this morning.
templates.resolutionText: {target_npc} sent word back. They asked when I was coming to visit, and they used the word 'when,' not 'if.' I've already started rehearsing what I'll say. Take {quest_reward}: I won't forget that you're the reason I get to be this nervous about something this good.

skillCheck.passText: I've been carrying a folded note in my pocket for three months. It's the words I would say if I could ever get them out. I haven't shown it to anyone, but I can tell you it exists.
skillCheck.failText: Let's just say it's been on my mind for a while. That's all there is to it.

templates.rewardText: a basket of the good things from my kitchen, and the biggest grin you've seen all week

templates.expositionText: My partner's mother does not approve of me, and she has made that very clear over the years. I want to ask for her blessing properly, before we take the next step, and I don't know how to begin. Please, would you talk to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}? They've known her since she was young, and they might tell you what would actually move her.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've been bracing myself for this for a year. It helps to have someone else in my corner.
templates.declineText: I see. Well, I appreciate you hearing me out, even if you can't help. Maybe I just need to find the courage on my own.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} said she once turned away a suitor over a slight nobody else even noticed? That tracks. But it also tells me she pays attention to gestures, even small ones, and that's something I can work with.
templates.conflict1Text: If she values gestures, then I need a real one. Find me a {quest_item}: the kind of thing you bring when you want someone to know you've thought about them, not just shown up. I'll present it when I make my case.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's just right. Not flashy, not cheap, just considered. Holding this, I almost feel like I might survive the conversation.
templates.conflict2Text: Last thing. Could you let {target_npc} know I'm coming to ask her properly next week? I'd rather she hear it from a friend first than be ambushed by me on her doorstep. It might soften the ground a little.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: {target_npc} agreed to put in a word? That's a kindness I won't forget. Now all I have to do is the actual asking, which somehow feels easier knowing the door isn't shut.
templates.resolutionText: She didn't say yes. She didn't say no, either: she said 'we'll see how you carry yourself this season,' and that's the most generous thing she's ever offered me. I'll take it. Please take {quest_reward}: you helped me get to a 'we'll see,' and that's a whole new horizon.

skillCheck.passText: You're right to ask: yes, my own parents had to win over an unwilling family. I grew up hearing the story, and I think part of why this one matters so much is because I know it can be done.
skillCheck.failText: It's an old family thing. I don't want to bore you with it.

templates.rewardText: a fine bottle I've been saving for the right occasion, and a hopeful heart

templates.expositionText: There's a tradition in my family for asking someone to share a life: you give two gifts, one practical and one foolish. My grandmother told me about it when I was a child, and now I finally have someone I want to use it for. I've been gathering the courage for a year. Please bring me a {quest_item}: something useful, something they'll reach for every day.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. It feels real now, in a way it hasn't before.
templates.declineText: That's all right. It's a private kind of help to ask for. I'll get there: I always meant to.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Yes. Yes, that's the practical half. Every time they pick this up, they'll think of me, and I want that very much.
templates.conflict1Text: Now the foolish half. Find me a {quest_item} that has no practical purpose at all: pretty, strange, the kind of thing that just makes someone smile. The whole point of the tradition is that you give one thing they need and one thing they'd never ask for.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Oh, that's wonderful. That is perfectly, perfectly foolish. They'll laugh, and that's exactly what my grandmother said the foolish gift was supposed to do.
templates.resolutionText: I haven't asked them yet: I will tomorrow morning, with both gifts in hand. But I already feel different than I did a week ago. Whatever they say, I tried, and that's a thing I never thought I'd be able to say. Take {quest_reward}: you gave me back a tradition I almost talked myself out of honoring.
templates.rewardText: a share of the good things I've been saving for my own celebration, whenever it comes

templates.expositionText: My husband has stopped sleeping. He sits up by the window all night and watches nothing, and when I ask him what he's looking for he says he doesn't know yet. He used to love a particular kind of warm drink his mother made him as a boy, and I think if I could put a {quest_item} in his hands again, it might reach him in a way I can't. Please find me one.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I haven't known what to do with my hands all week. At least now they're holding hope.
templates.declineText: I understand. He's not your problem to carry. I'll keep sitting up with him, and we'll be alright, one way or another.
templates.expositionTurnInText: You found one. He'll know what this is the moment he sees it: I just need to find the right moment to give it to him. Thank you for not asking me to explain why a small thing like this matters so much.
templates.conflict1Text: He drank a little of it, and then he talked for the first time in days. He told me he wants to hear from {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}, an old friend he hasn't seen in years. I think he's trying to put pieces of himself back together. Could you go to {target_npc} and tell them he's been asking?
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} is going to write to him? That's exactly what he needed to hear. Already he looks like a man who remembers he has a name.
templates.resolutionText: He slept last night. Not all the way through, but more than he has in weeks. He's not back yet, and I don't know if he's going to be the same when he is, but he's reachable now, and that's everything. Please take {quest_reward}: you brought my husband one step closer to home.

skillCheck.passText: The thing I haven't said out loud is that I think I missed the early signs. He was quieter for months before this, and I told myself it was just the season. I should have asked him sooner.
skillCheck.failText: I just need someone to help me try something. That's all.

templates.rewardText: a portion of our small savings and a wife's deepest thanks

templates.expositionText: My neighbor has been giving away her belongings, one piece at a time, to anyone who'll take them. She smiles when she does it. I don't think she means harm by it, but she's almost out of things, and she still seems to think she has too much. I need someone to talk to her sister: please find {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} and ask if this has happened before.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I've been afraid to make this real by saying it to anyone. Now it's real, and at least we can do something.
templates.declineText: That's alright. I'll try to talk to her myself, even though she doesn't really hear me. Some things you just have to keep at.
templates.expositionTurnInText: {target_npc} said she did this once before, after losing someone? Then I think I understand what she's doing now. She lost her partner this winter and she's been pretending it didn't break her. The giving-away is the breaking finally showing.
templates.conflict1Text: If she's grieving, then she needs something back, not more emptiness. Find me a {quest_item} that feels like a beginning: something a person uses when they're starting again, not finishing. I'll bring it to her and tell her it's from someone who cares about her.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: She held it for a long time without saying anything, and then she put it on her shelf instead of handing it back. That's the first thing she's kept in weeks. I want to cry just telling you about it.
templates.resolutionText: She came over for tea this morning. She didn't talk about her partner, but she didn't talk about giving things away either, and she sat with me for an hour. That's not better, not all the way, but it's not worse anymore. Take {quest_reward}: you helped me reach her when I couldn't reach her on my own.

skillCheck.passText: You're noticing that I haven't been sleeping much either, aren't you? It's hard to watch someone you love disappear in slow motion. I've been carrying her grief on top of my own.
skillCheck.failText: It's been a long week. I'd rather just focus on her right now.

templates.rewardText: the best of what little I have, and a thank-you I'll mean for a long time

templates.expositionText: My uncle hasn't left his home in nearly a month. He says the world outside is too loud, too fast, too much. He won't eat much of what I cook, but he used to love a particular {quest_item}, the kind that takes him back to when he was younger and steadier. Please bring me one: it's the only thing I can think to try.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. It's such a small thing to put my hope on, but small is all I have right now.
templates.declineText: I understand. He's not the easiest thing to explain, and you have your own life. I'll keep cooking and waiting, the way I have been.
templates.expositionTurnInText: He ate the whole thing. He didn't say much, but he ate the whole thing, and then he asked for a little more. That's the most I've gotten from him in weeks, and it's because of the {quest_item} you found.
templates.conflict1Text: He told me he wants to send word to {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement}, an old friend who used to visit. I don't know if he's ready to see anyone, but I think he's ready to know someone is still out there waiting for him. Could you go and tell {target_npc} my uncle was asking after them?
templates.conflict1TurnInText: {target_npc} took the news well? And they're going to write back? I almost don't recognize my uncle today: he asked me twice this morning if a letter had come yet.
templates.conflict2Text: There's one more thing he asked me for, quietly, like he was embarrassed. He wants a {quest_item}: something he used to keep on his table when he was younger and felt like himself. I think he's trying to remember who he was. Could you find one?
templates.conflict2TurnInText: He set it on the table exactly where he used to keep it. He just stared at it for a while, and then he smiled, just a little. I haven't seen that smile in a year.
templates.resolutionText: He went outside this morning. Only as far as the doorway, only for a few minutes, but he stood in the sun and he closed his eyes and he said it felt good. He's not all the way back, and I don't know if he ever will be, but he's still in there, and now I know how to reach him. Take {quest_reward}: you gave my uncle back to me one piece at a time.

skillCheck.passText: The truth is, I've been thinking I caused this. I pushed him to come to family gatherings he wasn't ready for, and after the last one he just went quiet. I'm afraid I'm the reason he stopped trying.
skillCheck.failText: He's family. That's the long and short of it.

templates.rewardText: a share of what my uncle and I can spare, and a niece's heartfelt thanks

templates.expositionText: My brother is dying. I haven't been able to get to him because the {enemy_type_plural} have been thick around here and I'm not the fighter I used to be. I need {kill_count} of them dealt with. I want to spend his last days at his side, not stuck in {settlement_name} listening to the wind.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I won't pretend this is easy to ask. Knowing you said yes makes the next part possible.
templates.declineText: I understand. It's a heavy thing to put on a stranger. I'll find another way, or I won't, and either way I'll live with it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: It's quieter now. I can hear myself think for the first time in days. There's something else I need before I can go to him, and I can't bring myself to face the looking.
templates.conflict1Text: He always kept a {quest_item} on him. I'd like one in my hand when I sit down beside him: something familiar, something he'd recognize even now. If one turns up out there, bring it to me. I can't go searching myself.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's the one. Or close enough that he'll know what I meant by it. I can feel my hands stop shaking just holding it.
templates.conflict2Text: There's one more thing, and it's the hardest. {target_npc} in {target_npc_settlement} should know. They were close to him once, before everything got complicated between them. Tell {target_npc_role} what's happening, in person. Don't soften it.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Then it's done. They know, and that's what mattered. I owed them that much, even if it cost something to send.
templates.resolutionText: I'm going to him tonight. {quest_reward} is yours, and I wish I had more to give for what you carried with me. I won't forget that I didn't have to do this alone.

skillCheck.passText: I keep telling myself I'm ready. I'm not. I don't know who I am after this, and I'm afraid to find out. But sitting here pretending isn't the same as being there.
skillCheck.failText: It's a hard road. That's all there is to say.

templates.rewardText: what coin I've set aside and a quiet thanks I won't take back

templates.expositionText: I have to send my daughter away. There's nothing left for her in {settlement_name}, and the {enemy_type_plural} prowling out there make any road north a death sentence as it stands. I need {kill_count} of them gone before I can even think about putting her on a wagon. Every day I wait makes the choice harder, and I'm already barely holding to it.
templates.acceptText: Then it's settled. I've been postponing this with every excuse I could find. Now I can't.
templates.declineText: I see. I can't fault you, truly. Some weights aren't yours to lift, and I shouldn't have looked at you like they might be.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Word came back that the way is clearer. I almost wish it weren't. There's still one more piece I can't manage from here, and it's the part I've been dreading most.
templates.conflict1Text: I can't be the one to arrange it. {target_npc}, the {target_npc_role} in {target_npc_settlement}, agreed years ago to take her in if it ever came to this. I need you to go and tell {target_npc} the time has come. They'll understand what to prepare.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Then it's real. I've been pretending this day was theoretical for so long, and now it's just a date on a calendar. I'd ask you to leave me a moment if you didn't already know I needed it.
templates.resolutionText: She leaves at the end of the week. I won't see her again for a long time, maybe ever, but she'll be safe and that has to be enough. Take {quest_reward}: it isn't much against what you helped me face, but I want you to have it.

skillCheck.passText: Can I tell you something I haven't said out loud? I'm not sure I'm strong enough to do this without flinching at the last moment. I keep thinking I'll find a reason to keep her here, and I know that reason would kill her eventually.
skillCheck.failText: It's a parent's burden. You'd understand it if it were yours.

templates.rewardText: a small purse and a piece of bread for the road

templates.expositionText: My wife asked me for one thing before she goes. A {quest_item}, the kind she used to keep when we were younger. I haven't been able to put one in her hand because I haven't been able to leave her side, and she doesn't have many days left. If you could find one for me, it would mean I kept my word at the end.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I'll tell her tonight that someone is looking. She'll smile, and that will be worth whatever this costs you to do.
templates.declineText: I understand. It's an odd thing to ask of a stranger, and I shouldn't have laid it on you. I'll keep watching the door and hoping.
templates.expositionTurnInText: She held it for a long time before she fell asleep. I haven't seen that look on her face in months. There's still more I need to manage before the end, and I can't do it from this chair.
templates.conflict1Text: I'd hate to ask this of you too, but the {enemy_type_plural} have been bold enough that I can't even step outside to fetch the doctor. Take {kill_count} of them down. I need to be able to reach the road when the time comes, without leaving her alone for hours.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: I went out for the first time in weeks and came back without my heart in my throat. That's a kindness I won't forget. There's one more thing, and it's the one I've been avoiding longest.
templates.conflict2Text: Her brother {target_npc} lives in {target_npc_settlement}. They haven't spoken in years, my fault as much as anyone's. He should hear it from a person, not a letter that arrives too late. Find {target_npc_role} and tell them plainly.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: He's coming. I didn't think he would. Whatever you said to him, it found the right place to land. She'll see him before the end.
templates.resolutionText: We have what time we have left, and now we can spend it without anything unsaid hanging over the room. {quest_reward} is yours, with my thanks. You gave us an ending I can live with.
templates.rewardText: a wood-carved keepsake from my workbench and a few coins beside

templates.expositionText: My husband passed last winter. I've made my peace with most of it, but there's a thing I can't quite let go of. He always wore a {quest_item}, and I lost the one he had on him in all the hurry afterward. If one turns up out there, even one that isn't his, I'd like to hold it again.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. It sounds like such a small thing to ask for, but it isn't, and I think you already know that.
templates.declineText: Of course. Not everyone wants to be tangled up in another person's grief. I'll be all right either way.
templates.expositionTurnInText: Oh. I haven't held one of these since the morning before he left. It even smells the same, which I know it shouldn't, but it does. There's one more thing left undone, and I think I can finally face it now.
templates.conflict1Text: His oldest friend {target_npc} lives in {target_npc_settlement}. I sent word back when it happened, but a letter isn't the same as someone telling you. I'd like {target_npc_role} to hear how it really went, from a person who can answer questions. Would you bring the news?
templates.conflict1TurnInText: Thank you. I should have done that myself, but I couldn't bear telling the story over and over to people who needed to ask. You carried that for me, and it was kind.
templates.resolutionText: I think I'll sleep tonight. Properly, for the first time in a while. Take {quest_reward}: it isn't payment, it's a thank-you for something that doesn't have a price.

skillCheck.passText: What I miss most isn't the big things. It's how he hummed when he didn't know I was listening. I keep catching myself listening for it in {settlement_name} and feeling foolish.
skillCheck.failText: It's been a long winter. I'd rather not say more than that.

templates.rewardText: a small ring of his that I have no use for now and won't see wasted

templates.expositionText: I lost my son to {enemy_type_plural} a season ago. I'm not asking for revenge: I'm past that. But every time I see another one of them slinking around {settlement_name}, I feel like the world hasn't moved on, and I need it to. Take {kill_count} of them down for me. Not for him. For me.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I knew when I asked that this wouldn't bring him back. That isn't what I'm looking for anymore.
templates.declineText: I understand. I shouldn't have made my grief into someone else's task. I'll keep trying to look the other way when I see them.
templates.expositionTurnInText: It's a different quiet now. Not better, exactly, but different. I caught myself thinking about something other than him for a whole afternoon, and that felt like a small mercy. There's something else I'd like to ask, while I still have the courage.
templates.conflict1Text: He always carried a {quest_item} when he went out. I never got the one he had with him back. I know it's silly, but if any one of them turns up in your travels, I'd like to put it on the shelf where his things sit. It doesn't have to be his. It just has to be the right kind.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That's enough. That's the one I'll keep. Looking at it doesn't hurt the way I thought it would, which is its own kind of strange.
templates.conflict2Text: There's one more thread. His old hunting partner {target_npc} lives over in {target_npc_settlement}, and I never told {target_npc_role} how it happened. They deserved better than the rumor that reached them. Go and tell {target_npc} the truth, if you would.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Thank you. They needed to hear it, and I needed to know they did. That's the last thing I was holding onto.
templates.resolutionText: I think I'm going to plant something this spring. I hadn't even thought about spring until today. {quest_reward} is yours, and I mean every bit of it: you helped me set down something I'd been carrying for too long.

skillCheck.passText: His grandfather was killed by {enemy_type_plural} too, before any of us were old enough to remember properly. I used to tell my son that story to make him careful. Now I wonder if I made him afraid of the wrong things.
skillCheck.failText: It's an old grief in this family. That's all I'll say tonight.

templates.rewardText: a few of his things that should belong to someone who'll use them

templates.expositionText: My mother passed two years ago this month. I've been meaning to mark the day properly and I keep putting it off. She used to carry a {quest_item} everywhere she went: said it brought her luck. I'd like to hold one again, just for the day. If one finds its way to you, bring it to me.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. It's small. It matters more than it should, and I think you understand that.
templates.declineText: That's all right. I've been carrying this on my own for a while. I can keep doing it.
templates.expositionTurnInText: There it is. She would have laughed at me for getting sentimental over something so plain. I'm going to set it on the table tonight and have a meal with her, the way we used to. There's one other thing, if you're willing.
templates.conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} have been close to {settlement_name} more often lately, and I can't sit with her memory in peace when I'm flinching at every sound outside. Take {kill_count} of them down. I want one quiet evening, that's all.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: It's the first quiet night I've had in weeks. I lit a candle for her and just sat with it. Nothing grand, but it was what I needed.
templates.resolutionText: I'll mark the day properly tomorrow. I haven't been able to do that in two years, and now I think I can. Take {quest_reward}: she would have wanted me to give something back to whoever helped.
templates.rewardText: the silver clasp she left me, which is no use sitting in a drawer

templates.expositionText: Something is wrong this season and I can't tell you what. The {enemy_type_plural} are showing up in numbers we haven't seen in years, in places they shouldn't even be. I'm not asking anyone to figure out why: I'm past that. I just need {kill_count} of them dealt with so we have a little room to breathe in {settlement_name}.
templates.acceptText: All right. I won't pretend I think it'll fix the bigger thing. But it's something, and something is more than I had this morning.
templates.declineText: I figured. Nobody knows what to make of any of this, and I can't blame you for not wanting to wade into it. We'll keep doing what we can on our own.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a real difference. I almost forgot what it felt like to walk to the well without watching the treeline. But the strangeness hasn't lifted, and we're running short on what we need to keep going.
templates.conflict1Text: We need {gather_count} {quest_item} just to get through the next stretch. I can't tell you why they've been so hard to come by lately, only that they have. Bring what you can. We'll stretch the rest.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: That'll hold us. I don't understand why what used to take an afternoon now takes weeks, but I'm grateful you went out anyway. There's one more thing pressing on me, and I'd be a fool not to ask while you're here.
templates.conflict2Text: The {enemy_type_plural} have been creeping back in, the same ones I thought we'd cleared. I don't know if it's the same group or a new one, and I've stopped trying to make sense of it. Take down {kill_count} more. We just need another stretch of quiet.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: Better. I won't say it's over, because I'm not sure anything is over, but it's better than it was. That counts for something tonight.
templates.resolutionText: I don't understand half of what's been happening this season, and I've stopped expecting to. But we're still standing, and that's more than I'd have wagered a month ago. Take {quest_reward}: I want you to have it before the next strange thing finds us.

skillCheck.passText: You've seen this kind of pattern before? That's almost a relief. I'd started thinking I was the only one who noticed they move differently when the wind shifts. Maybe we're not as blind as I thought.
skillCheck.failText: It's all guesswork at this point. I'd rather just deal with what's in front of us.

templates.rewardText: what we can spare from the storehouse and a warm meal besides

templates.expositionText: Things have been off for weeks and I can't put a name to it. Crops are thinner, the well rope frayed twice in a month, and I can't get my hands on anything I need. We need {gather_count} {quest_item} or we won't make it to the next harvest in {settlement_name}. I'd go myself, but I'm one person against a whole lot of nothing-going-right.
templates.acceptText: All right. Maybe with someone else trying, things will turn. They haven't yet, but maybe.
templates.declineText: I expected as much, honestly. I'll keep at it on my own. That's been the shape of this whole season anyway.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's more than I've been able to gather in a fortnight. I don't know what's been blocking me when it comes so easy to you, but I've stopped trying to puzzle it. There's one more thing pressing, and it's gotten worse while you were out.
templates.conflict1Text: The {enemy_type_plural} have been showing up where they shouldn't, and I don't know if it's connected to the rest of it or just bad luck on top of bad luck. Take down {kill_count} of them so I can sleep without one ear open. That's all I'm asking for tonight.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: I slept four hours straight. I haven't done that since this whole strange stretch began. Whatever's behind any of it, you've given me a piece of myself back.
templates.resolutionText: I won't tell you we're past it: I don't think I'd believe me if I said it. But things look a little less crooked than they did. Take {quest_reward}, and know that an ordinary stretch of days is the most I'm hoping for now.

skillCheck.passText: You see it too, then. The way the air's been wrong in the early morning, like it isn't holding the heat it should. I'd thought I was imagining it. Knowing I'm not is almost worse.
skillCheck.failText: It's a strange season. Strange seasons happen. I'm trying not to dwell.

templates.rewardText: a sack of what we have and the last good loaf out of my kitchen

templates.expositionText: I've stopped trying to make sense of any of it. {enemy_type_plural} where there shouldn't be, tools breaking that have lasted decades, the well tasting wrong for a week. I need {kill_count} of those things cleared out as a start. Just so I can stop bracing every time I open the door in {settlement_name}.
templates.acceptText: Thank you. I don't know if it'll help with the rest, but it'll help with this. I'll take what I can get this season.
templates.declineText: Fair enough. I've gotten used to expecting nothing. It's easier than the alternative.
templates.expositionTurnInText: That's a start. I won't pretend it's a solution, but the air feels less crowded. There's something else I've been chasing for weeks and getting nowhere with.
templates.conflict1Text: There's a {quest_item} I've been trying to find since this stretch started. I can't tell you why I can never get my hands on one anymore: they used to be everywhere. If you find one, bring it to me. It would be the first thing this month that went the way I wanted.
templates.conflict1TurnInText: There it is. I don't know whether to laugh or sit down. The smallest thing feels enormous when you've been failing at everything for so long.
templates.conflict2Text: We need to lay in {gather_count} {quest_item} too, while we have the chance. The way things have been going, I don't trust that we'll be able to get them next week. I'd rather have too much and look foolish than have nothing and be right.
templates.conflict2TurnInText: That's the most we've had on hand since this whole stretch began. I might actually breathe easy tonight, which would be a change worth marking.
templates.conflict3Text: And one more. The {enemy_type_plural} are creeping back in already, the same as before, and I don't know if they ever really left. Take {kill_count} more down. I keep thinking each round will be the last one, and I keep being wrong.
templates.conflict3TurnInText: Better. Quieter. I'm starting to wonder if sustained pushing back is the only answer when you can't see what you're pushing against. It's not understanding, but it's something.
templates.resolutionText: I don't know what made any of this happen and I don't think I ever will. But we're standing, and the next stretch of days might even feel ordinary. Take {quest_reward}: I wasn't sure I'd ever have anything to give back, and it feels good to be wrong about that.

skillCheck.passText: Honest answer? Fighting something I can't name has worn me down more than the work itself. I keep waiting to wake up and have it all make sense, and it never does, and I don't know how much longer I can keep getting up.
skillCheck.failText: It's been a long stretch. I'd rather not turn it over right now.

templates.rewardText: a fair share of what little we've managed to keep and my honest thanks
